extra.reason: Scaffold complete: added Go module, cmd/memento entrypoint, internal CLI dispatcher with help/version smoke behavior, Justfile test/build targets, and prefetched goldmark/yaml dependencies. Verified: just test; just build; /tmp/memento-build/memento help; /tmp/memento-build/memento version.

Implemented internal/vault marker discovery. Added Discover(repoRoot) for exactly-one .memento scans, Open(dir) for explicit vaults, manifest path resolution under .memento/manifest.json, and typed errors for missing or multiple markers. Tests cover single marker success, renamed vault directories, zero markers, multiple markers, and explicit-dir behavior. Verified with go test ./... and just check.

Implemented internal .mementoignore parser records for v0 comments/blanks, escaped leading hashes, root-relative patterns, file and directory patterns, segment globs, recursive ** segments, and unsupported ! negation errors. Verified with go test ./internal/ignore and just check.

Implemented parsed .mementoignore matching for vault-relative paths, including root-relative and anywhere file/directory rules, recursive directory matching, segment globs, recursive ** segments, and deterministic path normalization/rejection for absolute or escaping paths. Verified with go test ./internal/ignore and just check.

Added vault WalkMarkdown content walker that loads .mementoignore, prunes ignored directories, skips ignored files, excludes .memento/ and .mementoignore from content, and calls the extraction callback only for non-ignored .md files. Verified with go test ./internal/vault and just check.

Added internal markdown metadata extraction with goldmark-backed title/summary fallbacks, frontmatter tags/mode/updated parsing, body hash and summary staleness detection, plus malformed frontmatter errors. Verified with just check.

Added H2/H3 heading extraction to markdown metadata with level/text/slug fields, GitHub-style slug normalization, duplicate suffix handling including generated-slug collisions, and source-order preservation tests. Verified with go test ./internal/markdown and just check.
Added explicit summary hash coverage for no-frontmatter documents and body-change staleness detection. Verified with go test ./internal/markdown and just check.

Implemented deterministic manifest compilation and compile CLI. Added manifest JSON structs/marshalling, stable entry/tag ordering, idempotent manifest writes, compile --print/--dir behavior, and operational writing_guide.md exclusion from content walks. Verified with just check.

Implemented whole-file read by vault-relative key via new internal/note reader and CLI dispatch. Read honors the manifest content boundary through WalkMarkdown, returns nested markdown content, reports missing/ignored keys clearly, and rejects traversal/absolute/backslash keys. Verified with just check.

Implemented section reads for read <key>#<section>: split read targets, extract H2/H3 sections with manifest-compatible GitHub-style slugs and duplicate suffixes, preserve nested section boundaries, handle frontmatter offsets, and report unknown sections distinctly. Added note and CLI tests for exact heading text, normalized slug addressing, duplicate suffixes, frontmatter, nested sections, and clear failures. Verified with just check.

Implemented init filesystem scaffold/adoption: memento init now creates or adopts a vault, derives the default <project>-memory name from git remote or repo dir, creates .memento/config.toml, .memento/manifest.json, and .mementoignore only when absent, and preserves existing artifacts on rerun. Added CLI and setup tests for empty creation, non-empty adoption, no-clobber idempotence, and git-remote default naming. Verified with just check.

Implemented init bootloader injection for AGENTS.md/configured instruction files with sentinel append/replace behavior, resolved memory/manifest path interpolation, and idempotent reruns. Added setup tests for absent file, existing file append, existing block replacement, custom memory paths, configured instruction file, and idempotence. Verified with go test ./... and just check.

Implemented init pre-commit hook installation: absent hooks are created with #!/bin/sh and set -eu, existing hooks keep unrelated content, and existing memento blocks are replaced idempotently. Added setup tests for create/append/replace/idempotency. Verified with go test ./internal/setup and just check.

Implemented v0 write support: write now creates new markdown files and appends to existing notes from stdin, with invalid path, ignored/operational path, symlink traversal, and deferred overwrite/section/upsert mutation coverage. Verified with go test ./internal/note ./internal/cli ./internal/vault and just check.

Implemented compile-time wikilink graph extraction: added body wikilink scanner, structured outlinks with type/resolved state, target resolution to manifest keys, inlink inversion, deterministic dedupe/sorting, and spec note for the link schema. Verified with scanner and manifest unit coverage plus just check.

Implemented write-mode validation for append: existing read-only notes now return ErrReadOnly before mutation, malformed frontmatter blocks append, and non-read-only/missing modes still append. Verified with go test ./internal/note and just check.

Implemented robust compile frontmatter adoption behavior: horizontal-rule sandwich notes now index as bare markdown, compile uses lenient metadata extraction with fallback title/summary for malformed fenced frontmatter, and CLI compile emits warnings instead of hard-failing. Verified with go test ./internal/markdown ./internal/manifest ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented GitHub-style heading slug separator collapse. Changed headingSlug to collapse whitespace and hyphen separator runs, preserving duplicate -N disambiguation for normalized collisions. Added metadata coverage for double spaces, spaced hyphens, and mixed separator/punctuation runs; updated read-by-slug expectation to the collapsed anchor. Verified with go test ./internal/markdown, go test ./internal/note, and just check.

Implemented CLAUDE.md bootloader injection parity for init: default init now injects/replaces the sentinel block in every present AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md file, creates AGENTS.md only when neither exists, and preserves explicit AgentInstructionsPath behavior. Added setup tests for CLAUDE-only, both-files, replacement across both files, and rerun idempotence. Verified with go test ./internal/setup and just check.

Changed: init now writes an idempotent repo .gitignore Obsidian UI-noise stanza, removes Obsidian UI patterns from the default .mementoignore, and scaffolds example.md with model frontmatter only for greenfield vaults. Tests cover greenfield, adopt-existing-example, adopt-no-example, .gitignore idempotence, and CLI init expectations. Verified: go test ./internal/setup ./internal/cli; just check.

Implemented internal/brief renderer for deterministic agent-facing markdown with read-only frontmatter, generated banner, inline entry metadata, heading text, tag footer, and _memento tool-file footer. Updated compile to write .memento/manifest.json plus _memento/brief.md, warning without failing if brief write fails. Disabled HTML escaping in manifest JSON and skipped generated brief during vault walks. Verified with just check.

Implemented memento brief CLI verb. It reads existing _memento/brief.md, falls back to rendering from .memento/manifest.json and writing the derived brief, and reports a compile hint when both artifacts are missing. Added help text and CLI acceptance tests for all three states. Verified with go test ./internal/cli, just check, and go run ./cmd/memento brief.

Added manifest entry bytes and lines fields populated during compile from raw source size and newline count. Added coverage for empty files, no trailing newline, trailing LF, and CRLF; updated deterministic JSON fixtures and regenerated memento-memory/.memento/manifest.json. Verified with: just check.

Changed init bootloader generation to route agents through memento brief, kept the beads/memento substrate-boundary guidance, and removed writing_guide.md/writing.md guidance from generated blocks. Updated AGENTS.md dogfooding block and setup tests for AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md creation, append, replacement, idempotency, and absence of writing-guide references. Verified with: just check.

Changed: init now scaffolds <vault>/_memento/README.md before manifest creation, including read-only frontmatter, a memento-authored banner, and a sentinel-managed namespace convention block; existing README content is preserved while absent or stale managed blocks are appended/replaced. Tests cover greenfield, adopt-existing-no-README, adopt-existing-with-README, sentinel replacement, rerun idempotence, and manifest inclusion. Verified: go test ./internal/setup; go test ./internal/cli; just check.

Implemented init scaffolding for file-specific _memento/brief.md ignores: repo .gitignore sentinel blocks now include the generated brief path, .mementoignore defaults and adopted existing files add _memento/brief.md without ignoring the folder, and this repo's current ignore files were updated to match. Verified with just check.

Refreshed spec.md against accepted ADRs 0001/0002/0008/0009: bumped version to 0.2, updated vault naming/discovery, manifest+brief wording, _memento namespace, bootloader sample, v0-v4 scope table, and deferred list. Regenerated memento manifest so spec summary leads with post-ADR alignment. Verified with go run ./cmd/memento compile, go run ./cmd/memento brief, git diff --check, and just check.

Implemented a real memento serve dispatch stub that exits 1 with a not-implemented v3/spec §13 message instead of falling through as an unknown command. Added CLI regression coverage for serve ensuring no stdout, no unknown-command text, and the expected not-implemented context. Verified with go test ./internal/cli -count=1 and just check.

Replaced placeholder README with a concise project overview, WIP/pre-1.0 status, current CLI verbs including brief and serve stub, in-repo invocation examples, vault layout pointer, and links to spec/ADRs/AGENTS.md. Verified with just check.

Replaced the init-scaffolded _memento/README.md managed block with a write-once _memento/Using Memento.md guide. Added the guide to default/adopted .mementoignore handling so compile excludes it, refreshed tests for greenfield/adopt/no-op/re-init behavior, documented the design in ADR-0012, and refreshed the manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/setup and just check.

Implemented brief per-entry size markers from manifest Bytes/Lines with friendly byte formatting. Updated brief golden coverage for 342B, 1.2k, 12k, and zero-byte entries plus formatter coverage for 1.4M. Verified with go test ./internal/brief and just check.

Updated spec §10 to include memento brief; aligned serve help text with the v3 stub wording; added a focused help-text assertion. Verified with go test ./internal/cli -run TestHelpCommand -count=1 and just check.

Changed brief banner from hidden HTML comment to visible Obsidian caution callout; updated brief and CLI tests to expect the callout and reject the old HTML banner; amended ADR-0008 and regenerated the manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/brief ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented brief rendering per ADR-0011: manifest hash header on line 1, folder-grouped H2 sections with root group, continuous render-time numeric entry prefixes, and shared deterministic NumberedEntries ordering. Updated brief and CLI tests for the new projection. Verified with go test ./... and just check.

Implemented numeric read references for all-digit arguments. read N now resolves against .memento/manifest.json using brief.NumberedEntries, reads the resolved entry directly without compiling, reports the ADR-0011 stale-manifest message when the manifest entry file is missing, warns on brief/manifest hash drift, and skips the hash check when _memento/brief.md is absent. Updated CLI usage text and added CLI coverage for happy path, stale file, hash drift, missing brief, and non-numeric path behavior. Verified with just check.

Implemented brief-only wikilink index suffix rendering. Resolved summary wikilinks now render with @ N using the manifest wikilink resolver and brief numbering; display links use [[target|display @ N]], while broken and anchored links remain unchanged. Added renderer and CLI coverage, including source-file non-mutation during compile. Verified with go test ./internal/brief ./internal/manifest ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented explicit append-only default via markdown.DefaultWriteMode and used it when frontmatter omits mode. Added regression coverage for absent-mode metadata resolution, write overwrite rejection matching explicit append-only, and brief output showing mode: append-only for compiled bare markdown. Verified with just test and just check.

Implemented memento orient with embedded baseline plus manifest-selected orient:true overlays sorted by key. Added orient frontmatter parsing and manifest emission, CLI/help/spec entries, and regenerated the tracked manifest. Verified with just check and a real go run ./cmd/memento orient --dir memento-memory invocation.

Changed init bootloader block to the ADR-0013 pointer form referencing orient, brief, and read @N/key usage. Updated setup coverage for fresh init, replacement, custom paths, and configured instruction files. Added CLI regression coverage that compile, brief, orient, and read leave AGENTS.md unchanged after init. Verified with go test ./internal/setup ./internal/cli and just check.

Changed readNumberedEntry to read resolved numeric manifest keys through note.Read, preserving the stale-manifest hard error for note.ErrNotFound and deleting manifestEntryPath. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Added ADR-0016 as an accepted read-only ADR recording the @N CLI convention, brief wikilink display suffix convention, and brief manifest-hash frontmatter placement, with a scalar supersedes link back to ADR-0011 listing the affected paragraphs. Added a frontmatter forward pointer from ADR-0011 to ADR-0016 without changing ADR-0011 body content. Regenerated memento manifest/brief successfully. Verified with: go run ./cmd/memento compile; go run ./cmd/memento brief >/tmp/memento-brief-check.md; just check.

Moved brief manifest hash into frontmatter and updated drift parsing to read manifest: from the opening frontmatter block, independent of field order. Updated renderer/CLI tests for the new brief shape and parser edge cases. Verified with go test ./internal/brief ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented brief wikilink suffix rendering per ADR-0016: bare resolved wikilinks now render as [[target|target @N]], display-text links render as [[target|display @N]], and anchored/unresolved links remain unchanged. Updated focused brief and compile tests. Verified with go test ./internal/brief, go test ./internal/cli -run 'TestCompileBriefSuffixesWikiLinksWithoutMutatingSources|TestCompileArtifactsDoNotLeakHTMLEscapedSequences', and just check.

Changed memento read numeric references to require @N, with bare numeric-looking paths routed through normal key reads. Updated CLI help, tests for @N/stale/hash cases, invalid @ errors, literal 5.md path behavior, and spec §10. Verified with go test ./internal/cli, just check, and just run compile.

Added stable CLI error token formatting and exported manifest/CLI sentinels; documented the token contract in spec §10 and regenerated the manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Added manifest schema_version v1 emission and shared schema-aware manifest loading with manifest-schema-unsupported CLI token. Added v1 fixture round-trip, schema rejection, CLI token, and brief render contract order tests. Documented schema_version, brief render contract, and new error token in spec; regenerated memento-memory manifest. Verified with just check.

Changed: added .github/workflows/ci.yml with a ubuntu-latest + macos-latest matrix that installs Go from go.mod, installs just via apt/brew, and runs just check on push to main and pull_request; added the README CI badge.
Verified: just check passes locally; workflow YAML parses; a temporary deliberately failing Go test made just check exit nonzero, then was removed and just check passed again.
Note: attempted the requested linux container check, but Docker is unavailable in this sandbox (cannot connect to /var/run/docker.sock). The pushed workflow will provide the Linux/macOS runner signal.

Implemented compile performance coverage for the synthetic 500-document vault.

Changed:
- Added BenchmarkCompile500Docs and TestCompileWithin1s in internal/cli, using deterministic mixed frontmatter/bare markdown, 3-5 headings per doc, nested folders, and seeded wikilinks.
- Added just bench for go test -bench=. -benchmem ./internal/cli/.
- Documented the current benchmark baseline in README Performance: 18,019,817 ns/op on Apple M2 Max macOS arm64.

Verified:
- go test ./internal/cli -run ^TestCompileWithin1s$ -count=1 -v (23.847584ms observed)
- just bench (18,019,817 ns/op, 22.2 MB/op, 108,096 allocs/op)
- just check

v0 scope per spec §13 delivered. CLI verbs (compile/init/read/write/brief/orient + serve stub), .mementoignore subdir walk with glob+comment syntax, tag vocabulary, heading tree + GH-style slugs, out/in link graph as data, bare-markdown fallback, hash-based staleness detection, adopt-or-create init, pre-commit hook, sentinel bootloader injection (AGENTS.md + CLAUDE.md), .gitignore stanza, _memento/ namespace + Using Memento guide, brief render with manifest-hash frontmatter and @N numbering, README. 45/45 child beads complete. v1 hardening continues under memento-fb0.

Removed the memento serve stub from CLI dispatch/help, removed the serve-specific test and obsolete not-implemented sentinel, and updated README/spec/memory prose for ADR-0019's CLI-only roadmap. Re-homed read link surfaces to v2 and agent-driven summarisation to v4 in spec §13, refreshed the memento manifest with compile, and retitled/reframed parent epic memento-fb0. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Updated README CLI verb list to match memento help, including orient, --dir/--print usage, @N reads, and a short pointer to the spec error-token contract. Added a CLI regression test that compares README Current verbs against the help Usage/Commands output. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Changed: added focused vault-walk portability tests for NFC/NFD Unicode path spellings, case-only filename twins where the filesystem supports them, and symlinked files/directories; updated WalkMarkdown to skip all symlink entries; documented the v1 path-spelling and symlink policy in ADR-0020; regenerated the memento manifest. Verified: go test ./internal/vault; go test ./internal/manifest ./internal/note; just check.
Added CLI-level property tests for compile, brief, and init idempotency with deterministic generated vault/repo cases and shrink-on-failure reporting. Verified with just check.

Updated spec §15 with v1-close open-question triage: resolved shipped v1 items against ADRs, named v2 blockers, and parked deferred/post-v4 items from the open-thread notes. Regenerated memento-memory manifest. Verified with: go run ./cmd/memento compile --dir memento-memory; just check.

Completed the v1 agent-stability gate with three consecutive end-to-end validation runs.

Changed:
- Added commit-visible log exceptions for logs/memento-fb0.9-*.log while keeping normal Ralph logs ignored.
- Added run logs:
  - logs/memento-fb0.9-run-1.log
  - logs/memento-fb0.9-run-2.log
  - logs/memento-fb0.9-run-3.log

Each run built the CLI, compiled the project vault, read the brief, selected relevant durable docs via tag and heading scans, read the selected doc, performed a representative memento write in an isolated vault, compiled that vault, read the written note back, and exited with RESULT: PASS.

Verified:
- just check

Removed compile --print and non-init --dir flags; refactored CLI tests/benchmark/property tests to rely on cwd marker discovery; updated README/spec and regenerated manifest. Verified: go test ./internal/cli; go run ./cmd/memento compile; just check; git diff --check. Direct removed-flag smokes returned invalid-arguments. Direct init --dir smoke against the real repo was blocked by sandboxed .git/hooks writes, but init --dir remains covered by passing tests.

Implemented ADR-0017 v1 read-only edit-window slice: unratified read-only notes append successfully, ratified/non-git read-only notes still reject, and memento read emits binding: ratified|unratified on stderr before stdout content. Updated orient/bootloader text plus spec/ADR cross-references and recompiled the memento manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/note, go test ./internal/cli, just test, just check, git diff --check, and a CLI read smoke test.

Implemented explicit memento write --overwrite support. Added operation-aware mode enforcement for append/overwrite across ratified and unratified append-only/living/read-only notes, including summary_hash staleness coverage. Updated CLI help, README, orient baseline, spec, ADR-0017, and regenerated the memento manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/note ./internal/cli and just check.

Changed: added `description:` as summary fallback after `summary:` and before first paragraph; added explicit parsed-and-ignored OKF convention cases for `type`, `resource`, `timestamp`, and `okf_version`; updated ADR-0014, spec §9, and regenerated manifest metadata.
Verified: `go test ./internal/markdown`, `go test ./internal/manifest`, `just check`, and `go run ./cmd/memento compile`.

Implemented vault-rename-safe gitignore sentinel block: init now writes recursive **/.obsidian/workspace*, **/.obsidian/cache, and **/_memento/brief.md entries and removed the obsolete gitignoreVaultPrefix helper. Updated setup assertions and added a spec addendum documenting the recursive contract; refreshed the memento manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/setup, targeted init/idempotency tests, just run compile, and just check.

Changed: removed the unused manifest_path default from init-created config.toml, added a setup test asserting header-only config contents, updated spec.md to describe config.toml as a header-only extension point, and recompiled the memento manifest.
Verified: go test ./internal/setup; just check.

Documented vault rename policy in spec.md: bare memento init rediscovers the moved marker, refreshes the AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md bootloader and pre-commit hook, and replaces older path-pinned gitignore blocks while the current recursive gitignore block survives renames. Verified with go run ./cmd/memento compile and just check.

Changed ratification git checks to use global --literal-pathspecs for worktree detection and ls-files, so vault keys with pathspec metacharacters are checked literally. Added regression coverage for a tracked bracket filename returning ratified and an untracked wildcard filename not being ratified by a different tracked match. Verified with: go test ./internal/note -run 'TestBindingForKey(TreatsTrackedPathspecMetacharactersLiterally|DoesNotRatifyUntrackedPathspecMetacharacterMatch)' -count=1; just check.

Removed the unreachable exec.ErrNotFound ratification fallback so unexpected git ls-files exec failures now return contextual errors instead of ratifying. Added a regression test for git disappearing after the work-tree probe. Verified with go test ./internal/note and just check.

Changed ratification git probing to capture stderr and surface fatal/broken repository failures instead of treating them as non-git or untracked. Preserved the conservative non-git fallback for old Git's 'not a git repository' output when no .git marker exists in the vault ancestry, and added focused shim tests for old non-repo, broken repo rev-parse, fatal rev-parse, and fatal ls-files behavior. Verified with go test ./internal/note -run 'TestBindingForKey', go test ./internal/note, git diff --check, and just check.

Changed validateWriteMode to check ratification before reading/parsing metadata, so unratified writes return early. Added regression coverage for unratified malformed frontmatter to prove metadata parsing is skipped. Verified with go test ./internal/note and just check.

Inlined BindingForKey into BindingForReadTarget and removed the redundant normalizeKey call from isRatified. Updated ratification tests to exercise BindingForReadTarget directly. Verified with go test ./internal/note and just check.

Removed the derivable checkBriefHash local from runRead and now gates warnIfBriefHashDrift directly on strings.HasPrefix(target, "@"). Verified with just fmt and just check (gofmt, go test ./..., go vet ./..., go build).
Changed CLI test cwd helpers to report restore failures via testing.TB Fatalf instead of ignoring os.Chdir errors. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.
Closed

Implemented ADR-0022 auto-compile after successful memento write. Write now regenerates manifest and brief via the compile artifact path, emits compiled entry count on stderr, skips compile on rejected writes, and returns non-zero with a warning if post-write compile fails while leaving the body on disk. Updated write CLI/orient/README descriptions and added CLI tests for success, failed-write skip, compile-failure asymmetry, and sequential writes. Verified with go test ./internal/note ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented ADR-0010 writing guide surfacing. Orient now conditionally shows the memento write precondition when _memento/writing.md exists; greenfield init scaffolds a minimal read-only writing guide while adoption never creates or clobbers it; write remains silent. Added dogfooded memento-memory/_memento/writing.md and recompiled the manifest after running memento init against the non-empty repo vault. Verified with focused orient/init/write tests and just check.

Implemented Path B. Anchored wikilinks now parse into a separate anchor field, manifest outlinks and inverted inlinks serialize anchor when present while keeping schema_version 1, and duplicate suppression preserves distinct anchors. Added anchored-link manifest fixture coverage plus extractor coverage. Verified: go test ./internal/markdown ./internal/manifest; go test ./internal/cli -run TestCompileIdempotencyProperty; just check.

Implemented whole-file read stderr link surface from the manifest. Added CLI coverage for stdout byte preservation, fixed role ordering, empty-role omission, resolved @N rendering, unresolved raw targets, @N reads, empty-link docs, and wiki/embed separation. Updated orient/bootloader/README wording. Verified with go test ./internal/setup ./internal/orient ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented section-scoped read link surfaces. Section reads now re-extract outgoing wikilinks/embeds from the section excerpt, filter incoming wiki/embed links by manifest anchor against the resolved section slug, and keep whole-file read behavior unchanged. Runtime path in effect: anchor-filtered section inlinks; file-scoped fallback remains covered by an explicit helper test. Verified with targeted go test ./internal/cli -run 'TestRead(Section|WholeFile|EmptySection)' and just check.

Changed runCompile to emit compiled: <N> entries on stderr after successful artifact writes, using the same count/format as write. Updated compile, write parity, idempotency, and compile performance tests for the new stderr contract. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Replaced orient triggered-preconditions literal placeholder with a stable embedded marker. baselineForVault now renders the marker as either the writing-guide precondition or 'None yet.' and errors if the marker is missing/duplicated. Added an orient unit test asserting the baseline marker exists and updated CLI orient expectations for rendered baseline output. Verified with go test ./internal/orient ./internal/cli and just check.

Restored wikilink extraction to skip bare same-doc anchors () so they do not become unresolved outlinks. Added parser and CLI regression coverage, updated manifest fixture expectations, amended ADR-0021 with the decision, and refreshed the memento manifest. Verified with ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/markdown	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/manifest	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/cli	(cached), , and ?   	github.com/tpisel/memento/cmd/memento	[no test files]
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/brief	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/cli	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/ignore	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/manifest	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/markdown	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/note	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/orient	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/setup	(cached)
ok  	github.com/tpisel/memento/internal/vault	(cached).

Replaced hardcoded wiki/embed link-type comparisons in internal/cli/cli.go with markdown.WikiLinkTypeWiki and markdown.WikiLinkTypeEmbed constants; helper parameters now use markdown.WikiLinkType directly. Verified with just check.

Changed read to emit a missing-manifest link-surface hint after binding while preserving stdout and zero exit behavior. Updated no-manifest read tests to pin the hint and absence of role lines. Verified with focused go test for read cases and just check.

Changed memento write post-write compile failures to return exit 3 with a recovery warning while preserving exit 1 for write failures. Updated the regression test, ADR-0022 wording, and regenerated the memory manifest. Verified with targeted go test and just check.

All 11 children closed cleanly. Shipped: ADR-0010 (writing.md orient conditional precondition), ADR-0021 (read-time link surface — whole-file + section-scoped), ADR-0022 (auto-compile after successful write, with exit code 3 for partial-success write-OK-compile-failed state). Follow-ups landed: compiled-entries stderr line for verb parity (.6), templated orient precondition marker (.7), wikilink same-doc anchor decision (.8), link-type constants over string literals (.9), missing-manifest hint on read link surface (.10), distinct exit code 3 for write/compile partial success (.11). All on main; just check green at each commit; tests added throughout.

Implemented write guardrails and success echo. note.Write now returns the resolved absolute path; write rejects keys prefixed with the vault basename using the invalid-key token and suggestion; CLI emits wrote: <abs path> (<byte count>, append|overwrite) before compile status, including partial-success compile failures. Updated spec durable context and manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/note ./internal/cli and just check.

Added .github/workflows/release.yml for v* tag pushes only. It grants contents: write, checks out full history, sets up Go from go.mod, and runs goreleaser/goreleaser-action@v6 with release --clean plus GITHUB_TOKEN and HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN.

Verified: ruby YAML parse for release.yml/ci.yml/.goreleaser.yaml; git diff --check; just check. actionlint/yamllint were attempted but unavailable in this sandbox (command not found / Python module missing); go-run actionlint fetch was blocked by restricted network DNS.

Updated init bootloader text to lead with durable-knowledge value, require orient then brief, note brief compactness/no head piping, and direct agents to read over grep/cat with stderr link metadata and key#heading extraction. Regenerated this repo's AGENTS.md managed block to match. Updated setup bootloader tests. Verified: go test ./internal/setup; just check.

Updated orient baseline wording for overlays, summary_hash ownership, and section-read syntax. Added dynamic brief disclosure rendered from manifest entry count and brief line estimate, including no-notes fallback. Covered marker substitution, populated disclosure, and empty-manifest disclosure in orient tests; updated CLI exact-output expectations. Verified with go test ./internal/orient ./internal/cli and just check.

Changed pre-commit hook rendering to guard memento compile/staging with command -v and warn/exit 0 when memento is unavailable. Updated init hook rendering/idempotency assertions and added a shell execution test for absent PATH. Verified with go test ./internal/setup and just check.

Updated README Install to list Homebrew, go install, and Releases download in the requested order; kept Quickstart and Development scoped as-is. Verified with install-section grep/readback and just check.

Smoke-tested README quickstart on fresh git repos under /private/tmp using this checkout installed into a temporary GOBIN.

Changed: README quickstart now uses `decisions/storage-choice.md` for both `memento write` and `memento read`, matching the CLI's required markdown key shape.

Verified after the README fix: `memento init` exited 0, created `smoke-memory/`, created AGENTS.md from nothing with the memento bootloader, wrote the `.gitignore` stanza, and installed an executable pre-commit hook. `memento write decisions/storage-choice.md` exited 0 and auto-compiled; `memento brief` listed `decisions/storage-choice.md`; `memento read decisions/storage-choice.md` returned the note body on stdout and `binding: unratified` on stderr.

Found behavior drift: the generated pre-commit hook runs `memento compile --dir 'smoke-memory'`, but current CLI rejects `--dir`, so the next commit exits 1. Filed sibling bug `memento-34u.6` with observed-vs-expected details.

Verification: `just check` passed.

Fixed init-generated pre-commit hook to run supported `memento compile` form without unsupported --dir flag while still staging the resolved manifest path. Added setup coverage for generated hook content plus an end-to-end git commit smoke using the current CLI binary, and appended a spec addendum documenting the hook command contract. Verified with `go test ./internal/setup`, `go run ./cmd/memento compile`, and `just check`.

Added release-notes-v0.1.0.md at the repo root with a 287-word v0.1.0 GitHub Release draft covering the what-is framing, v0-v2 shipped CLI surface, stable error tokens, manifest schema v1 caveat, install commands, README quickstart pointer, and v0.2+ not-yet items. Verified with wc -w release-notes-v0.1.0.md, git status --short, and just check.

Changed brief rendering to use @N entry headers, omit empty tags metadata and empty Headings lines, and add the section-read footer hint. Kept Summary: none intentionally as an explicit missing-summary marker. Updated brief/CLI tests, appended the spec addendum, and regenerated the manifest with just run compile. Verified: go test ./internal/brief ./internal/cli; just check.

Implemented ADR-0023 summary-staleness ledger: manifest schema v2 with carried body/summary hashes and derived current/stale/missing state; markdown exposes SummaryState; brief renders stale/unsummarised markers plus footer counts; read stderr emits summary state; orientation baseline updated; dogfooding manifest regenerated. Verified with go test ./..., go run ./cmd/memento compile, go run ./cmd/memento read @33, and just check.

Implemented CLI-side key-not-found suggestions for read: basename and .md-stem matches from the manifest, case-insensitive variants, @N labels, max three suggestions, and stale/missing manifest fallback. Added CLI tests for suggestion, cap/case-insensitive matching, no-suggestion fallback, and stale manifest suppression. Verified with go test ./internal/cli -run 'TestReadSuggests|TestReadMissingKey|TestReadFailsClearlyForMissingOrIgnoredKey' and just check.

Implemented @N#heading reads by parsing the section suffix after numeric manifest resolution and reusing the existing section reader. Aligned bootloader/orient wording and regenerated the repo AGENTS.md managed block text. Added CLI coverage for @N#heading parity with key#heading plus invalid @abc#heading token preservation. Verified with go test ./internal/cli ./internal/setup ./internal/orient ./internal/brief, just check, and manual go run ./cmd/memento read '@9#Brief render contract'.

Added a top-level context-injection discipline principle to AGENTS.md covering bootloader blocks, orient text, brief footers, error messages, and stderr metadata. CLAUDE.md is not present, so no mirror was needed. Verified with just check.

Implemented write warning for first-time top-level vault directories using the pre-write manifest snapshot and resolved write path. Added CLI coverage for warning order, no warning for existing top-level manifest entries, and no warning for root-level files. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Acceptance already satisfied in current tree: release-notes-v0.1.0.md is absent from .gitignore, remains tracked at repo root, git check-ignore exits 1, and git status is clean. Verified with just check.

Changed bootloader and orient brief wording from compact to dense; updated setup/orient/CLI golden expectations and synced this repo's AGENTS.md sentinel block. Verified with go test ./internal/orient ./internal/setup, targeted compact-wording rg, live go run ./cmd/memento orient, and just check.

Implemented ADR-0023 polish: brief now switches on markdown summary-state constants; prior ledger fallback is documented and covered by a v1 reseed test; manifest transition coverage now includes stale->stale and missing->current; Metadata.SummaryHash was renamed to SummaryTextHash and callers/tests updated. Verified with go test ./internal/manifest ./internal/markdown ./internal/brief ./internal/note and just check.

Split internal/cli/cli.go into per-verb files: brief.go, compile.go, init.go, orient.go, read.go, and write.go. Kept Run/RunWithInput and shared vault/manifest helpers in cli.go; errors.go unchanged. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Verified the existing GoReleaser v0.1.0 config against the remaining acceptance gates.

Cleanup:

Verified by read-only sweep: no .md file under memento-memory/ still carries summary_hash: in frontmatter (0 of 43). Remaining 6 mentions are prose references in ADR-0014/0018/0023, spec.md, OKF note, and brief.md — correct to keep. Cleanup already landed.

Changed orient baseline/disclosure so `brief` is on-demand for doc-landscape scans, not mandatory.

Updated orient/setup/cli tests for the new wording and regression checks against old mandatory brief phrasing.
Verified: go test ./internal/orient ./internal/setup; just check.

Added inactive Claude Code SessionStart orient hook at scripts/agent-hooks/orient-session-start.sh. The script resolves repo root from its path, prefers an installed memento, falls back to go run ./cmd/memento orient, and emits Claude hook JSON with orient output as additionalContext. Usage comment includes the .claude/settings.json SessionStart snippet and resume/compact matcher note. Verified by parsing hand-run hook JSON and by running just check.

Added inactive scripts/agent-hooks/pre-write-vault-guard.sh with Claude PreToolUse usage snippet, vault-root env/arg support, marker discovery, and deny/no-op behavior for vault vs non-vault Write/Edit/MultiEdit paths. Added Go execution tests under internal/agenthooks. Verified: go test ./internal/agenthooks; hand-run script denies memento-memory/spec.md and allows README.md; just check.

Added memento-memory/a-uat/test-matrix.md via memento write. The matrix covers Claude Opus, Claude Sonnet, and Codex; all eight hook/skill variation arms; desired behaviors B1-B5; concrete prompt/circumstance/expected-action pairs; n=3 guidance; runnable Codex scoping to skill-only arms while Claude hook artifacts remain Claude Code format. Verified with memento read a-uat/test-matrix.md and just check.

Implemented scoped -h/--help for read/write/brief/orient/compile/init. Added shared subcommand flag parsing that sends help to stdout with exit 0 and preserves invalid-arguments errors otherwise. Added table-driven CLI tests covering -h and --help for every scoped verb, including read @N/key#heading/stderr metadata and write key/overwrite/mode/edit-window/wrote-path contracts. Verified with go test ./internal/cli and just check.

Changed write vault-prefix guard to use strings.EqualFold so differently-cased vault basenames are rejected as repo-relative input. Added note-layer coverage for same-case and mixed-case vault-prefixed keys, plus CLI coverage asserting mixed-case input returns invalid-key and creates no nested file. Verified: go test ./internal/note -run TestWriteRejectsVaultPrefixedKey; go test ./internal/cli -run 'TestWriteRejects.*VaultPrefixedKey'; just check.

All 8 children closed cleanly (8/8). v0.1.0 distribution wiring shipped; release gate passed. Final agent-doable blocker (34u.7 macOS case-insensitive vault guard) verified fixed via strings.EqualFold + tests; just check green (tests/vet/build). Human-only items (tap repo, token, tag/push, clean-machine brew verify) tracked separately in release-checklist.md.

All 3 children closed (3/3): lean orient + brief-pull, orient SessionStart hook artifact, pre-write vault-guard. Default-install / skill-adoption / write-enforcement decisions remain gated on A-UAT (memento-7kr matrix, now populated).

Implemented Claude setup defaults: init now scaffolds _memento/skills/write.md, installs .claude/skills/memento-write/SKILL.md from that vault source, installs/updates a repo-local Claude SessionStart orient script, and merges the SessionStart hook into .claude/settings.json while preserving unrelated settings/hooks/skills. The hook runs memento compile before memento orient and still emits orient output with a concise compile-failure note if compile fails. Updated the reusable Claude hook artifact and made the checked-in write skill wording agent-generic; recompiled the manifest. Verified with go test ./internal/setup ./internal/cli and just check.

Implemented memento write --force-with-reason for explicit protected-write overrides. Empty/whitespace reasons now reject as invalid arguments before stdin/write; forced mode bypass is limited to ratified read-only append/overwrite and append-only overwrite rejections; forced successes emit forced: true and reason on stderr while non-protected writes keep existing output. Added CLI and note tests for protected rejection, empty reason rejection, forced append/overwrite, non-protected behavior, and no bypass for key validation, malformed protected metadata, or stdin read errors. Verified: go test ./internal/note; go test ./internal/cli; just check.

Implemented the vault write guard expansion: Write/Edit/MultiEdit denials now cover all native file tools with protected-note force guidance, Bash PreToolUse events deny obvious vault writes via redirection, tee, cp, mv, sed -i, and perl -pi, and the A-UAT guard matcher now includes Bash. Added hook tests for native denials, non-vault allows, dynamic vault discovery, each denied Bash write form, and allowed memento read/brief/orient/compile/write commands. Verified with go test ./internal/agenthooks, go test ./internal/setup ./internal/agenthooks, bash -n scripts/agent-hooks/pre-write-vault-guard.sh scripts/a-uat/run-cell.sh, just check, and git diff --check.

Updated init for the operational _memento namespace (ADR-0029/0030).

Changed (internal/setup/init.go):
- defaultIgnore now ignores _memento/ structurally (replaces the per-file _memento/brief.md and _memento/Using Memento.md entries).
- ensureMementoIgnore adoption path now ensures a single structural _memento/ entry instead of the two file-specific lines; idempotent.
- Added ensureConventionTemplates: greenfield/adoption both scaffold _memento/conventions/{writing,summarising,conventions}.md with title + when_to_read frontmatter only, project-neutral bodies, preserving any existing convention file.

Tests (internal/setup/init_test.go, internal/cli/cli_test.go): updated ignore/skill-manifest assertions to the structural policy; added greenfield-creation, project-neutrality, adoption-preservation, idempotence, and "conventions absent from manifest" coverage.

Verified: just check (go test ./..., go vet, build) passes. Manual: memento init scaffolds the three conventions, .mementoignore has structural _memento/, manifest indexes only example.md (no _memento/* files), and compile still regenerates _memento/brief.md via the direct operational reader.

Out of scope (other 960 beads): orient convention rendering (.2), memento convention verb (.4), migrating the legacy _memento/writing.md + skill references (.5). The legacy greenfield _memento/writing.md and walk.go brief exemption are left for .5.

Added memento convention <name> read verb and validation (ADR-0029).

Changed:
- internal/convention/convention.go (new): Read(v, name) validates the name as a bare lowercase filename stem (no slash, extension, space, or traversal → ErrInvalidName), reads _memento/conventions/<name>.md (absent → ErrNotFound naming the rel path), splits frontmatter, requires non-empty when_to_read (else ErrInvalid), and returns the body without frontmatter. Exposes Path/RelPath/ValidateName for reuse by orient (960.2).
- internal/markdown/metadata.go: exported SplitFrontmatter wrapping splitFrontmatterBlock.
- internal/cli/convention.go (new): runConvention prints the body; arity errors → exit 2 (invalid-arguments), convention errors → exit 1.
- internal/cli/cli.go: dispatch + help Usage/Commands entries for convention.
- internal/cli/help.go: conventionHelpText.
- internal/cli/errors.go: tokens invalid-convention-name, convention-not-found, invalid-convention.
- README.md: CLI reference entry (keeps README/help parity test green).

Tests: internal/convention/convention_test.go (frontmatter stripping, quoted scalars, invalid names, missing file, missing/empty when_to_read, no-frontmatter→invalid); internal/cli/cli_test.go (subcommand help + functional success, invalid name, missing file, missing/empty when_to_read, arity).

Verified: just check passes (go test ./..., go vet, build). Manual smoke: convention writing prints body sans frontmatter (exit 0); absent → convention-not-found naming _memento/conventions/absent.md (exit 1); writing.md → invalid-convention-name (exit 1); no when_to_read → invalid-convention (exit 1); help lists the verb.

Out of scope (other 960 beads): orient rendering of conventions (960.2), migrating legacy _memento/writing.md and skill refs (960.5). No memento-memory change: implementation follows ADR-0029 as written; nothing durable beyond it.

Render When To Read Conventions block in orient (ADR-0029).

Changed:
- internal/convention/convention.go: add List(v) — scans _memento/conventions/*.md, returns valid conventions sorted by Name plus one warning string per invalid file (bad name or missing/empty when_to_read); missing dir yields empty, non-md files skipped, not an error.
- internal/orient/orient.go: Render now returns (out, warnings, err); appends a '## When To Read Conventions' section listing each valid convention as '- <when_to_read>: `memento convention <name>`', deterministic by name; block omitted when no valid conventions; invalid files surface as warnings without suppressing valid ones.
- internal/cli/orient.go: prints each warning to stderr as 'memento orient: warning: <msg>'; updated to new Render signature.
- internal/orient/orient_test.go, internal/convention/convention_test.go, internal/cli/cli_test.go: tests.

Tests: orient — three valid defaults sorted, no-conventions omits block, invalid convention warns but keeps valid; convention.List — sorted listing, invalid-warns/non-md-skipped, missing-dir-empty; cli — end-to-end block on stdout + warning naming the bad file on stderr.

Verified: just check passes (go test ./..., go vet, build). Manual smoke in an init'd vault: orient lists conventions/summarising/writing sorted, no warnings, conventions excluded from brief corpus (1 note); broken convention → stderr warning, valid retained.

Out of scope (960.5): legacy Triggered Preconditions writing-guide line still points at _memento/writing.md. No memento-memory change: follows ADR-0029; _memento brief-corpus exclusion already covered by ADR-0030/init .mementoignore.

Regression-confirmed the A-UAT agent write policies; all claims have coverage. Verified existing tests: force-with-reason (note: TestWriteForceWithReason* + TestWriteRejectsRatifiedReadOnlyModeWithoutChangingFile; cli: TestWriteRejectsRatifiedReadOnlyModeWithoutForce, TestWriteOverwriteRejectsRatifiedAppendOnlyMode, TestWriteRejectsEmptyForceReasonBeforeWriting, TestWriteForceWithReasonAppends/Overwrites/DoesNotChangeNonProtected + key-validation/malformed/stdin bypass guards); vault-root-scoped hook denials/allows (agenthooks: TestPreWriteVaultGuardDeniesVaultWrite, AllowsNonVaultWrite, DiscoversVaultRoot); Bash write detection (agenthooks: TestPreWriteVaultGuardDeniesObviousBashWritesIntoVault covering >, >>, tee, cp, mv, sed -i, perl -pi, plus AllowsBashOutsideVaultAndMementoCommands); orient compile-before-orient (setup: TestClaudeOrientHookRunsCompileBeforeOrient); compile-failure fallback (setup: TestClaudeOrientHookRunsOrientWhenCompileFails). Added one narrow missing test for the only gap: TestInitWriteSkillInstallIsIdempotent (rerun Init keeps _memento/skills/write.md and .claude/skills/memento-write/SKILL.md byte-identical). No product behavior changed. Verified: go test ./internal/setup -run TestInitWriteSkillInstallIsIdempotent; just check (go test ./..., go vet ./..., build all pass).

Migrated the writing guide and write-skill references from _memento/writing.md to the writing convention.

Changes:
- orient: removed the Triggered Preconditions section and its hard-coded `memento read _memento/writing.md` line (baseline.md + orient.go: dropped triggeredPreconditionsMarker, hasWritingGuide, markerName). The writing prompt now flows solely through the When To Read Conventions block (`memento convention writing`), per ADR-0029.
- write skill generator (defaultWriteSkill): step 1 now runs `memento convention writing`; source-of-truth line points to the writing convention (_memento/conventions/writing.md).
- init: removed greenfield creation of _memento/writing.md (ensureWritingGuide + defaultWritingGuide); the convention template under _memento/conventions/writing.md is now the only scaffolded writing guide.
- Preserved existing user-authored _memento/writing.md on adopt (criterion #4) -- init never touches it. No fallback to the old path remains in orient or skill text.
- Updated repo's dogfooded _memento/skills/write.md to match the new generator.

Tests: added TestOrientNeverReferencesLegacyWritingGuidePath, TestOrientRoutesWritingPromptThroughConvention, TestBaselineHasNoLegacyWritingGuidePrecondition, TestInitDoesNotCreateLegacyWritingGuideForGreenfieldVault; write-skill test asserts convention path and rejects legacy refs.

Verified: just fmt/vet/build clean; full `go test ./...` passes; live `memento orient` shows no Triggered Preconditions / _memento/writing.md and routes writing via `memento convention writing`. Note: TestCompileWithin1s is a machine-load-dependent perf threshold (flaky under suite load, passes in isolation), unrelated to this change.

Discovery (follow-up, not this bead): the generated brief still lists _memento/writing.md via the legacy tool-files footer (brief.DetectToolFiles/RenderWithToolFiles, ADR-0010). That separate surface and the Using Memento.md "tool-read files" prose were left unmigrated; worth a bead under the ADR-0030 namespace cleanup.

Added end-to-end regression test TestConventionsEndToEndFromInit in internal/cli/cli_test.go covering the full ADR-0029/0030 flow against an init-produced vault: init scaffolds the three default conventions (writing/summarising/conventions); orient lists each as a 'memento convention <name>' prompt; the convention verb returns body-only content (frontmatter stripped) for all three; the normal brief and manifest exclude _memento operational files; and a malformed convention (missing when_to_read) is surfaced both as an orient warning and an invalid-convention verb error. Existing component-level coverage (convention pkg, setup/init templates, orient rendering, convention verb) was already thorough, so only this integrating test was added. Verified: 'go test ./internal/cli/ -run TestConventionsEndToEndFromInit' (PASS) and 'just check' (fmt+test+vet+build all green).

Migrated defaultUsingMementoGuide onboarding prose (internal/setup/init.go) off the legacy top-level _memento/writing.md|review.md|audit.md tool-read model. New prose describes the conventions namespace: _memento/conventions/ guides declare when_to_read:, surfaced by memento orient and loaded via 'memento convention <name>' (consistent with ADR-0029/0030). Updated assertUsingMementoGuide in init_test.go to require the conventions/'memento convention <name>' prose and to reject the legacy 'Tool-read files such as', review.md, and audit.md strings. Existing user-authored Using Memento.md still preserved on adopt (unchanged ensureUsingMementoGuide + TestInitDoesNotModifyExistingUsingMementoGuide). Verified: go test ./internal/setup/ (pass), just check (all packages pass, vet+build clean).

Stopped brief from surfacing legacy _memento/ tool files (ADR-0030).

Changed:
- internal/brief/brief.go: removed DetectToolFiles, toolConsumedFileNames, RenderWithToolFiles, inlineToolFiles, and the "Tool files:" brief footer line. Render now builds the projection directly; dropped now-unused imports (errors, os, path/filepath, vault).
- internal/cli/compile.go: writeBriefArtifact now calls brief.Render(m) directly, dropping the _memento/ tool-file detection step.
- internal/brief/brief_test.go: removed the "Tool files: none" footer expectations and the DetectToolFiles/RenderWithToolFiles tests; added TestRenderDoesNotSurfaceOperationalToolFiles asserting brief never emits "Tool files:" or _memento/audit/review/writing markers.

No legitimate non-_memento tool-file surfacing existed (the only detected files were _memento/ paths), so the footer was removed outright rather than repurposed.

Verified: just check (gofmt, go test ./... all pass incl. brief + cli integration, go vet, go build).

Deduped convention scalar parsing. Added exported markdown.FrontmatterScalar (raw-frontmatter key lookup reusing the unexported cleanScalar); convention.Read now calls it and the private frontmatterScalar/unquoteScalar reimplementations were removed. Added markdown.FrontmatterScalar unit test (plain/double/single-quoted/comment/empty/absent/nil). Verified: go test ./internal/convention/ and ./internal/markdown/ pass, go vet clean, gofmt clean. No behavior change. (Unrelated flaky TestCompileWithin1s perf benchmark passed on re-run.)

Documented + tested block-scalar handling for convention when_to_read (ADR-0029 single-line-scalar intent kept; rejection chosen).

Finding: a block scalar `when_to_read: |` / `>` did NOT yield "" -> ErrInvalid as the bead assumed; FrontmatterScalar returned the bare indicator ("|"/">") as a non-empty value, so the convention was silently ACCEPTED with garbage when_to_read. Worse than the described limitation.

Changes:
- internal/markdown/metadata.go: FrontmatterScalar now treats a lone YAML block-scalar header (leading |/> plus optional +/-/1-9 modifiers) as absent (returns ""), via new isBlockScalarHeader helper; doc comment explains why. A value with real text after the indicator (e.g. "> redirect") stays a value.
- internal/convention/convention_test.go: TestReadBlockScalarWhenToReadIsInvalid asserts |, >, |-, >+, |2 -> ErrInvalid (CLI token invalid-convention preserved via existing errors.go mapping).
- internal/markdown/metadata_test.go: TestFrontmatterScalarRejectsBlockScalar covers the helper directly incl. the non-indicator case.
- memento-memory ADR-0029: added Validation bullet stating when_to_read must be single-line; block scalars are unsupported/invalid.

Verified: just check (go test ./..., go vet, build) all pass.

Done in commit f46d8cd. memento convention <name> reads _memento/conventions/<name>.md body-only with name validation and convention-specific not-found/invalid errors. Verified: just check passes; live 'memento convention writing' prints body only; 'memento convention bogus' returns convention-not-found exit 1.

Done in commit a9d6158. orient renders deterministic 'When To Read Conventions' block; invalid conventions warn without suppressing valid ones; block omitted when none. Verified: just check passes; live orient lists conventions/summarising/writing in name order.

Done in commit f1b56be (earlier Ralph interruption resolved; commit present and verified). init scaffolds _memento/conventions/{writing,summarising,conventions}.md and .mementoignore now ignores _memento/ structurally. Verified: just check passes (setup tests); live brief excludes all _memento keys.

Done in commits aa772b1 + f64b87f. Write guidance moved to _memento/conventions/writing.md; skill/orient copy references 'memento convention writing'; manifest regenerated. Legacy _memento/writing.md retired in ccc8383. Verified: just check passes.

Done in commit 450f805. End-to-end regression coverage for convention parsing, orient rendering, init templates, and brief exclusion. Verified: just check passes (convention, orient, setup, cli, vault, markdown suites green).
Done in commit 3629672. Added narrow regression coverage (internal/setup/init_test.go) for A-UAT-derived write policies. Verified: just check passes (agenthooks, setup, cli suites green).
Done in commit 97a69de. Using Memento.md onboarding prose migrated to conventions model (points at memento convention writing). Verified: just check passes.

Done in commit 42a9392 (structural walker skip) + 4076ffe/bcc2aee. brief/manifest no longer list _memento/ files. Verified: live compile yields 44 entries, zero keys under _memento/; brief shows no _memento keys or 'Tool files' line.
Done in commit a8208e4. convention package reuses markdown.FrontmatterScalar/SplitFrontmatter; no duplicated scalar parser. Verified: just check passes (markdown + convention suites).
Done in commit b0c6550. Block-scalar when_to_read documented as invalid in ADR-0029 and covered by isBlockScalarHeader + tests. Verified: just check passes.

All children complete (ADR-0029 + ADR-0030 implemented). orient lists writing/summarising/conventions prompts and routes to 'memento convention <name>'; _memento/ is a structurally-ignored operational namespace. Verified end to end: just check passes; live orient/convention/brief confirmed against the dogfood vault.

All four children complete (.1-.3 implementation + .4 regression). Evidence-gated agent integration defaults landed: orient hook + write skill install, vault write-routing guard, force-with-reason override, regression coverage. Verified: just check passes.

Fixed in commit e6119f3. resolve_path normalizes to forward-slash separators; the vault-guard file_path glob now matches on Windows. Verified: CI run 28219102288 green on windows-latest, macos-latest, ubuntu-latest; local go test ./internal/agenthooks passes.

Tightened write-mode lattice to three nodes per ADR-0031/ADR-0015.

Changed:
- internal/markdown/metadata.go: deleted retired ModeSectionReplace/ModeKeyedUpsert constants; validMode now accepts exactly {ModeAppendOnly, ModeLiving, ModeReadOnly}. Lenient parse path stays graceful (invalid mode surfaces as frontmatter error, falls back to empty frontmatter).
- internal/markdown/metadata_test.go: rich-markdown fixture now uses mode: living (asserts ModeLiving); added rejection cases asserting section-replace and keyed-upsert parse to ErrInvalidMode (frontmatter-invalid).

Verified:
- just check green (gofmt, go test ./... all pass, go vet, build).
- grep of internal+cmd shows no remaining ModeSectionReplace/ModeKeyedUpsert references.

Out of scope (separate beads): internal/note/write.go still carries OperationSectionReplace/OperationKeyedUpsert (WriteOperation type, distinct identifiers not matched by the acceptance grep).

Extracted pure, side-effect-free enforcement primitives into new internal/enforce package (ADR-0031 salvage list).

Added:
- enforce/key.go  NormalizeWritableKey: writable-key rules (vault-prefix, non-.md, operational paths, ignored) over note.NormalizeKey; reads ignore rules, never mutates FS. Owns ErrVaultPrefixedKey.
- enforce/path.go ResolveWritablePath: salvaged writablePath MINUS its os.MkdirAll side-effect. Containment via deepestExistingDir (climbs to nearest existing ancestor + EvalSymlinks) so non-existent parents resolve without creating anything.
- enforce/mode.go EvaluateMode: validateWriteMode's lattice refactored to return Decision{Allow, ReasonCode, Message} instead of (forced, error). Reason codes read_only / append_only_overwrite per ADR-0031 denial taxonomy. Pure: ratification/grant/force composed by caller.
- enforce/ratify.go IsRatified: thin delegate to note.IsRatified (no git mechanics forked).

Reuse without duplication: exported note.isRatified->IsRatified and note.normalizeKey->NormalizeKey (callers in note/read.go, write.go, ratification.go updated). Dependency is one-directional enforce->note (no cycle); write.go's own copies left intact so the write verb is unchanged.

Tests: enforce unit tests for key normalization, path resolution incl. an explicit "does not create parent directories" assertion, three-mode lattice, and ratification (non-git/committed/untracked). 31 cases.

Verified: just check green (fmt, test, vet, build); note package tests still pass (write verb unchanged).

Added the write-mode porcelain verb (ADR-0031 surviving-verbs).

Changes:
- internal/markdown/metadata.go: ParseWriteMode (rejects unknown modes via b1's tightened validMode, closing ADR-0015's typo open-question) and SetMode (rewrites/inserts the frontmatter mode: line, preserving other fields and body; creates frontmatter when absent).
- internal/cli/write_mode.go: runWriteMode dispatch. Resolves key via enforce.NormalizeWritableKey/ResolveWritablePath, reads old mode, enforces loosening-requires-justification (lattice rank read-only<append-only<living) while tightening accepts it as optional, writes the mode line, then recompiles for manifest coherence. parseInterspersedFlags lets --justification trail the positionals as documented.
- internal/cli/cli.go: write-mode dispatch + top-level help Usage/Commands entries.
- internal/cli/help.go: writeModeHelpText.
- README.md: CLI reference entry (kept in sync with help, enforced by TestReadmeCurrentVerbsMatchHelp).

Verified (just check green): markdown SetMode/ParseWriteMode unit tests; CLI integration tests cover set+recompile (manifest mode updated), unknown-mode rejection (exit 2, note unchanged), loosening-requires-justification then succeeds with it, tightening with/without justification, missing-note (key-not-found), and arity. Note: TestCompileWithin1s is a pre-existing CPU-calibrated latency gate that flakes under load; orthogonal to this change and passes in just check.

Added the unlock verb and grant sidecar (ADR-0031).

Changed:
- internal/enforce/grant.go (+grant_test.go): grant lifecycle helpers consumed by check-write and the pre-commit hook. Grant{Justification, GrantedAt}; GrantsPath; LoadGrants (read/list, missing=empty map, malformed=error); LookupGrant (single-key, for the mutability predicate); AddGrant (create/refresh, merges so concurrent grants survive, caller-supplied timestamp for determinism); ClearGrants (idempotent remove-all = the re-lock).
- internal/cli/unlock.go (+unlock_test.go): 'memento unlock <key> --justification <reason>'. Requires non-empty justification, normalizes the writable key, requires the note to exist, writes the grant with time.Now().UTC(). No recompile (gitignored sidecar is not vault corpus).
- internal/cli/cli.go, help.go: dispatch + help text; top-level usage/command list.
- internal/setup/init.go: gitignore block gains file-scoped '**/.memento/unlock-grants.json'; manifest.json/config under .memento stay tracked (no folder-wide ignore).
- README.md: verb-list entry (README/help parity test).

Verified: go build ./...; go vet ./... clean; gofmt clean. enforce + setup packages green. CLI unlock/write-mode/gitignore/readme/help tests green. grant lifecycle (create/read/lookup/merge/clear/missing/malformed) covered.

Not from this bead: 'just check' shows TestCompileWithin1s failing (1.0-3.8s vs <1s budget across runs) — a load-sensitive compile-latency benchmark. This change touches nothing in the compile path and unlock deliberately skips recompile; the failure tracks machine contention, not this work. ADR-0031 already flags tightening that bench as separate follow-up.

check-write core landed (Write derivation, prefix invariant, verdict JSON).

Changed:
- internal/enforce/invariant.go: EvaluatePrefixInvariant (read-only ≡ new==old; append-only ≡ new has old as prefix; living allow) + EvaluateVaultWrite composing the mutability predicate (new-note creation ∪ unratified ∪ active unlock grant short-circuit to allow; otherwise the invariant). New reason code append_only_interior.
- internal/cli/check_write.go: hidden 'check-write' verb. Reads raw PreToolUse JSON on stdin, resolves abs file_path → vault key (symlink-safe even before the target exists), reads old-bytes from disk + ratification from git + active grants itself, derives new-bytes (Write=content verbatim; Edit/MultiEdit report errUnsupportedDerivation for b6), evaluates, emits full harness verdict JSON on stdout {decision→permissionDecision, message→permissionDecisionReason, reason_code top-level}. allow auto-approves in-vault; out-of-vault/non-write tool = silent exit 0; internal error = non-zero exit (wrapper fails closed in b10). vault_discovery_ambiguous emits ask.
- internal/cli/cli.go: dispatch case (not in help text).
- internal/enforce/key.go: NormalizeWritableKey now rejects _memento/ (ToolDirName), not just .memento/ — closes a gap so the unwritable_path taxonomy matches ADR-0031; benefits write-mode/unlock too. Added test cases.
- memento-memory/check-write output contract.md: durable note resolving ADR-0031's ambiguous output wording (full harness JSON on stdout, not a bare struct), for b10/b18/b19.

Verified: go test ./internal/enforce/ and ./internal/cli/ green. Table tests cover US1 (ratified read-only edit denied→read_only), US2 (truncating Write denied→append_only_overwrite, prefix-extending Write allowed), US5 (new note incl. mode allowed), US6 (unratified allowed), plus active-grant reopen, unwritable_path, out-of-vault inert, non-file-tool inert, ambiguous-vault ask, unsupported-derivation non-zero exit, and help-omission. go vet/build/gofmt clean. Sole suite failure is the pre-existing flaky TestCompileWithin1s timing bench (passes in isolation; unrelated to this change). memento compile green (48 entries).

Added Edit/MultiEdit new-bytes derivation to check-write (ADR-0031 US3).

Changed:
- internal/enforce/replay.go: ReplayEdits(old, exists, []Edit) replays Claude's apply algorithm in Go — sequential edits each against the prior result, replace_all substitutes all occurrences, non-unique match aborts (ErrReplayAmbiguous) unless replace_all, missing match aborts (ErrReplayNoMatch), creation reserved to Write (ErrReplayCreateViaEdit on absent file or empty old_string).
- internal/cli/check_write.go: preToolUse now decodes old_string/new_string/replace_all and the MultiEdit edits array; deriveNewBytes routes Edit/MultiEdit through ReplayEdits; brokenPrefixReason attaches append_only_interior for in-place edits vs append_only_overwrite for Write; replay aborts on in-vault targets fail closed (exit non-zero).

Verified (just check green):
- internal/enforce/replay_test.go golden fixtures pin disk-old+edits -> exact landed bytes (unique replace, tail-append, interior rewrite, replace_all, sequential MultiEdit, chained edits) and the abort cases match Claude (no-match, ambiguous, create-via-edit, empty old_string, abort surfacing from a later edit).
- internal/cli US3 test: append-only interior Edit denied (append_only_interior), tail-append Edit allowed; underivable Edit fails closed.

No memento-memory change: ADR-0031 already documents the replay caveat and golden-fixture oracle. Bash/codex apply_patch derivation remain later beads.
